patterns.where: Anchor Bay (NW corner) — heavy weeds + pads
patterns.details: Anchor Bay is the shallow weed-flat sister to the open lake. Largemouth water at scale.

patterns.title: Winter perch ice fishing
patterns.technique: Jigging minnow + waxworm rigs; tip-downs
patterns.where: Off Selfridge + the Mile Roads
patterns.details: Lake St. Clair ice scene is huge. Perch quality varies cyclically.

regulations: MI license valid; near international waters in spots. Specific musky regs (36" min). Bass tournament regs may apply.
notes: The undisputed #1 musky water in North America. Smallmouth fishery is also world-class. Charter scene is full-time. The Mile Roads are submerged road beds (built before the lake flooded them) that create perfect structure.

name: Detroit River
signatureSpecies: Walleye, smallmouth bass, white bass, lake sturgeon

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: World-class spring walleye run. The Detroit River walleye run is one of the most-fished events in the Great Lakes.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run.

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Catch + release. Below Belle Isle.

patterns.title: Spring walleye run
patterns.when: March-May (peak last week March through early May)
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging with 3/8-3/4 oz jigheads + plastic minnows (gulp, swim minnows); hair jigs with stinger hooks
patterns.where: Trenton Channel; off Lake Erie Metropark; below Wyandotte; Belle Isle area; the Livingstone Channel
patterns.details: THE Great Lakes walleye event. Tens of thousands of fish stack in the river to spawn in March-April. Jig vertically in 30-60 ft. Hire a charter — local knowledge matters.

patterns.title: Trophy white bass spring schools
patterns.technique: Casting small jigs + spinners + Cleos into schooling fish
patterns.where: Trenton Channel + off Wyandotte

regulations: Spring walleye special regulations may apply. Sturgeon catch + release only. Border with Canada — US license OK in US waters.
managementProgram: MI DNR + Ontario MNRF cooperative walleye management
notes: The connector between Lake St. Clair and Lake Erie. Spring walleye run is one of the most famous fish migrations in North America.

name: St. Clair River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring spawn run from Lake St. Clair into Lake Huron.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Heavy current = trophy smallmouth on bottom structure.

species.name: Lake sturgeon
species.notes: Catch + release.

patterns.title: Spring + fall walleye vertical jigging
patterns.technique: Vertical jigging 1/2-1 oz jigheads in heavy current
patterns.where: Below Algonac; the St. Clair Flats delta area

notes: Connects Lake Huron + Lake St. Clair. Strong-current river with trophy walleye + sturgeon.

name: Saginaw Bay (Lake Huron)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: One of the most productive walleye fisheries in the Great Lakes. Self-sustaining population.

species.name: Yellow perch
species.notes: World-class jumbo perch when populations cycle high.

patterns.title: Trolling walleye on the open bay
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + bottom bouncers; trolling Reef Runners + Husky Jerks on planer boards
patterns.where: Inner Bay flats 12-22 ft; the Outer Bay 25-40 ft in summer
patterns.details: Saginaw Bay walleye fishery is genuinely world-class. Multiple year-class booms in recent years. Charter scene is robust out of Bay City + Caseville + Linwood.

patterns.title: Jumbo perch summer + fall
patterns.technique: Crappie minnows + emerald shiners on perch rigs; small jigs + waxworms
patterns.where: Mid-bay over hard bottoms; the gravel shoals

managementProgram: MI DNR Saginaw Bay walleye management
notes: Inner Bay is shallow + warm (10-30 ft); Outer Bay is deeper + colder. Walleye + perch fishery is the heart of MI Lake Huron angling.

name: Saginaw River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring walleye run from Saginaw Bay.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run.

patterns.title: Spring walleye run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 3/8-1/2 oz; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Bay City + below the Center Avenue Dam in Saginaw
patterns.details: Saginaw walleye stage to spawn in late March-April. Less famous than Detroit but quality.

notes: Formed by confluence of the Tittabawassee + Shiawassee + Cass + Flint rivers. Drains the largest watershed in MI.

name: Tittabawassee River

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Spring run staging here is iconic — fish move up from Saginaw Bay.

patterns.title: Spring walleye below Sanford Dam
patterns.technique: Jigging plastics + minnows; small swimbaits
patterns.where: Below the (rebuilt) Sanford Dam; Midland area
patterns.details: The Sanford + Tittabawassee dams failed catastrophically in 2020. Rebuilding ongoing. Walleye habitat is shifting as the system recovers.

notes: The "Tittabawassee" run is part of the Saginaw walleye system. 2020 dam failure changed the river's character — recovery is ongoing.

name: Grand River
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, walleye, smallmouth bass

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Michigan to the 6th Street Dam in Grand Rapids.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Spring + fall runs.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below dams (Lyons, Lansing).

patterns.title: Fall salmon at 6th Street Dam (Grand Rapids)
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners; bobbers + skein
patterns.where: Below 6th Street Dam in downtown Grand Rapids
patterns.details: Salmon stage below the 6th Street Dam in the heart of downtown Grand Rapids. Urban salmon fishing — most-fished hole in the SLP.

patterns.title: Spring walleye below dams
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/4 oz
patterns.where: Below Lyons + Portland + Lansing dams

notes: MI's longest river. Flows from Jackson through Lansing + Grand Rapids to Lake Michigan at Grand Haven. Urban salmon fishery in downtown GR.

name: Kalamazoo River
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, smallmouth bass, brown trout

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up to Allegan Dam.

patterns.title: Salmon below Allegan Dam
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners
patterns.where: Allegan Dam tailwater

notes: Tailwater fishing below Allegan Dam. Recovery from Enbridge oil spill (2010) is ongoing.

name: St. Joseph River (Berrien County)
signatureSpecies: King salmon, steelhead, brown trout, smallmouth bass

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up to Berrien Springs Dam.

species.name: Steelhead
species.notes: Skamania + winter strain.

patterns.title: Salmon staging at Berrien Springs Dam
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners; spey casting
patterns.where: Berrien Springs Dam tailwater

notes: NOT the same St. Joseph River as the South Bend one (different watershed). This one drains to Lake Michigan at St. Joseph, MI.

name: Au Sable River — Lower (Oscoda)

species.name: King salmon (chinook)
species.notes: Fall run up from Lake Huron.

patterns.title: Fall Au Sable salmon (Foote Dam)
patterns.technique: Spawn sacs + spinners
patterns.where: Below Foote Dam (Iosco County) downstream to Oscoda
patterns.details: The Lake Huron side of the Au Sable. Foote Dam is the limit of upstream salmon passage. Less crowded than Lake Michigan tribs.

notes: The Au Sable's lower 12 miles below Foote Dam is salmon + walleye water. Distinct from the Holy Water (upper Au Sable trout system).

name: South Holston River (below SoHo Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Premier trophy brown trout tailwater in the Southeast — possibly in the country. Heavy class fish are common, 24+ in regular.

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Wild + stocked. Very strong population.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.size: Lower river, warm water below the trout zone.

patterns.title: Sulfur hatch dry-fly window
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun; CDC sulfur cripple; long-fine leaders 6X-7X
patterns.where: Throughout the 14-mi tailwater; classic riffle-to-pool transitions
patterns.details: The SoHo sulfur hatch is the signature dry-fly event in TN. Fish rise selectively — match emerger vs. dun vs. cripple. The brown trout get genuinely picky. Dam generation schedule matters — fish during wadeable low-flow windows.

patterns.title: Midge dry + emerger fishing
patterns.technique: Size 22-26 zebra midge nymph; size 24-26 midge dry (Griffith's gnat cluster); RS-2 emerger
patterns.where: Slow flat water + tailouts
patterns.details: When generation is OFF and water is clear, SoHo trout sip midges in unprecedented numbers. 7X tippet, perfect drift. The most technical small-fly fishery in TN.

patterns.title: Streamer fishing trophy browns
patterns.technique: Galloup's Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Sculpzilla; smaller patterns when water is low + clear
patterns.where: Deep banks + log jams + tailwater current seams
patterns.details: SoHo browns hit articulated meat hardest in cold low-light periods. Wading + drift-boat both work. 7-8 wt rod for the trophy class.

patterns.title: Scud + sowbug nymphing (year-round)
patterns.technique: Size 14-18 grey/pink scud; size 14-16 sowbug; under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater on any moving water
patterns.details: Scuds + sowbugs are the year-round food forms in TN tailwaters. When you don't know what they're on, drift these. Pink scud is the SoHo specialty.

patterns.title: Dam generation timing
patterns.technique: Fish low-flow windows for wade fishing; high-flow windows for drift boats
patterns.where: TVA posts schedule daily at tva.com/lakes/Generation-Schedules
patterns.details: TVA generates South Holston Dam for hydropower. When generation is on, the river rises 3-4 ft + becomes float-only. Wade-fishing windows are critical — check schedule before driving. Generation timing also drives bug activity.

regulations: Slot limit on brown trout: protect 16-22 in. 7-fish daily creel. Catch-and-release zones in the trophy area.
managementProgram: TWRA wild brown trout management — slot limit
notes: The South Holston is THE Southern brown trout tailwater. 14 miles below SoHo Dam in Sullivan County. The brown trout slot limit (protecting 16-22 in fish) is the foundation of the trophy fishery.

name: Watauga River (below Wilbur Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Like the SoHo — trophy brown water with strict regs.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked.

patterns.title: Sulfur hatch (same as SoHo but slightly different timing)
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater
patterns.details: Watauga sulfur hatch is on the same schedule as the SoHo. Same technical match-the-hatch fishery.

patterns.title: Streamer browns + slot-protection
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers; smaller patterns than the SoHo in tight water
patterns.where: Below Wilbur Dam

patterns.title: Midge + scud nymphing
patterns.technique: Zebra midge + scud + sowbug under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout

regulations: Brown trout slot limit (protect 16-22 in). 7-fish creel.
notes: The Watauga is the SoHo's twin — same fishery character, same regs, similar fish. Less crowded but every bit as good.

name: Clinch River (below Norris Dam)

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked + holdover wild fish.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Trophy class — Clinch is famous for very large browns when conditions align.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river warm zone.

patterns.title: Midge nymphing (the Clinch signature)
patterns.technique: Size 20-26 zebra midge (black, red, olive); UV2-Pheasant tail tiny; under indicator with split-shot
patterns.where: Throughout the 13-mi tailwater
patterns.details: The Clinch is a midge-fishery. Generations limited periods of midge clouds. 7X tippet, indicator nymphing, multiple-flies rig with weight. The most technical small-fly TN tailwater.

patterns.title: Sulfur evening dry fly
patterns.technique: Size 16-18 sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout

patterns.title: Streamer big browns
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers; smaller patterns when low/clear
patterns.where: Deep banks + below structure
patterns.details: Clinch browns get truly large (30+ in documented). Pre-spawn fall fish are the prize.

patterns.title: Wooly Bugger for wading anglers
patterns.technique: Olive Wooly Bugger size 8-10 stripped or swung
patterns.where: Throughout — works for wading anglers without fly gear depth
patterns.details: When in doubt, throw an olive Wooly Bugger on the Clinch. Even very large trout will eat it.

regulations: Slot limit + 7-fish creel.
notes: Most-fished TN tailwater for its proximity to Knoxville. 13 miles of cold tailwater below Norris Dam.

name: Caney Fork River (below Center Hill Dam)

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Heavily stocked TWRA + wild reproduction.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Trophy class — Caney browns are famous, especially in the upper tailwater zone.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Below the trout zone toward Carthage; warmwater character.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Migrate up from the Cumberland.

patterns.title: Year-round midge + scud nymphing
patterns.technique: Size 20-22 zebra midge; size 14-16 scud (pink, olive); under indicator
patterns.where: Throughout the 28-mi tailwater
patterns.details: Caney Fork is a year-round nymph fishery. The cold dam discharges keep trout active all season. Pink scud is a regional standout — TVA dam discharge color-shifts the natural scuds.

patterns.title: Caddis + sulfur dry-fly hatches
patterns.technique: Size 14-18 caddis (Elk Hair Caddis, X-Caddis); sulfur emerger + dun in late spring
patterns.where: Riffle-to-pool transitions in the upper tailwater

patterns.title: Streamer fishing trophy browns
patterns.technique: Galloup's Sex Dungeon, Boogie Man, Kreelex (flashabou attractor)
patterns.where: Deep banks below the dam; the trophy zone above Happy Hollow
patterns.details: Caney browns key off shad + sculpin forage. Kreelex (Chuck Kraft, regional) is the flashabou pattern that catches everything on this water.

patterns.title: Junk-fly drift for stocked rainbows
patterns.technique: Mop Fly, Squirmy Wormy, Y2K, San Juan Worm
patterns.where: Throughout — but especially the public-access points
patterns.details: TWRA stocks the Caney heavily through the year. Junk flies clean up on fresh stockers.

patterns.title: Center Hill Dam generation timing
patterns.technique: Watch the TVA generation schedule
patterns.where: tva.com/Energy/Our-Power-System/Hydroelectric/Center-Hill
patterns.details: Center Hill Dam runs unpredictably. Best wade-fishing windows are early morning before generation kicks in + late summer afternoons when load drops. Always check the schedule + listen for the dam siren.

regulations: Slot limit + 7-fish creel. Some catch-and-release zones above Happy Hollow.
notes: The Caney Fork is Middle Tennessee's flagship tailwater. 28 miles of cold water below Center Hill Dam. Drains to the Cumberland near Carthage.

name: Hiwassee River (below Apalachia Powerhouse)
signatureSpecies: Rainbow trout, brown trout, smallmouth bass

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Stocked + reproducing.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river + warm zone — unusual to have both trout + bass.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Spring run from Chickamauga Lake.

patterns.title: Yellow jacket bank-fishing summer dry
patterns.technique: Size 12-14 Yellow Jacket pattern (foam, yellow-and-black banded)
patterns.where: Bank-side wherever overhanging trees + flowers attract bees
patterns.details: The Hiwassee's yellow jacket bite is genuinely unique to this water. Bees fall off bankside flowers + bushes in midsummer afternoons. Fish rise to them like terrestrials. Bank-slap technique.

patterns.title: Streamer + dry-dropper through the rapids
patterns.technique: Articulated white streamer (fall/winter delayed harvest); dry-dropper with Stimulator + caddis dropper
patterns.where: The Hiwassee's class II-III rapids
patterns.details: The Hiwassee is unique among TN tailwaters — it has serious whitewater. Float by kayak or raft. Fall delayed-harvest is the most-celebrated window for big browns.

patterns.title: Smallmouth in the lower river
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned rigs, in-line spinners, surface poppers
patterns.where: Below the cold-water zone toward Reliance + Etowah

regulations: Standard trout regs. Delayed-harvest section October-March.
notes: The Hiwassee is a hybrid trout/smallmouth/whitewater river. Most-paddled trout water in the Southeast. The fall + winter delayed-harvest is a destination season.

name: Holston River (below Cherokee Dam)
signatureSpecies: Rainbow trout, brown trout, striped bass

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: TWRA stocked.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Migrate up from Cherokee Lake in summer for cool tailwater discharge. World-record class fishery for stripers in trout water.

patterns.title: Summer striped bass on flies
patterns.technique: Big white streamers (Lefty's Deceiver, Half and Half, Bob Popovics Hollow Fleye); 9-10 wt rod with sink tip
patterns.where: Cool tailwater pools below Cherokee Dam where stripers stage during summer thermal-refuge migration
patterns.details: World-class warmwater fly fishing — the trout-water stripers of the Holston are an East TN specialty. 25-40 lb fish hit articulated baitfish in 60-65°F water while everyone else is dredging deep lake holes.

patterns.title: Trout nymphing + dry-fly
patterns.technique: Midge nymphs + scud + sulfur dry-fly in season
patterns.where: Upper tailwater immediately below Cherokee Dam

regulations: Striped bass with TN reciprocity license + trout stamp where applicable.
notes: The Holston is the rare tailwater with both trout AND world-class striped bass — driven by thermal refuge migration from Cherokee Lake in summer.

name: Elk River (below Tims Ford Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Strong wild brown population — Elk browns are quality.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Lower river warm zone.

patterns.title: Sulfur dry + emerger
patterns.technique: Sulfur emerger + dun size 16-18
patterns.where: Throughout the tailwater

patterns.title: Wading scud + midge nymphing
patterns.technique: Size 14-16 scud + size 20-22 midge under indicator
patterns.where: Wadeable stretches below the dam

notes: Less-crowded Middle TN tailwater. Quality fishery for those willing to drive past Caney Fork. Tims Ford Dam discharge.

name: Obey River (below Dale Hollow Dam)

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: World-record class historically — Dale Hollow Lake holds the IGFA world-record smallmouth, and the Obey tailwater browns are part of the same cold-water system.

patterns.title: Streamer trophy browns
patterns.technique: Articulated streamers
patterns.where: Deep pools below Dale Hollow Dam

patterns.title: Sulfur evening hatch
patterns.technique: Sulfur emerger + dun
patterns.where: Throughout

notes: Possibly the most-isolated TN tailwater — 90+ mi from any major city. Trophy browns + low pressure.

name: Norris Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, smallmouth bass, walleye, lake trout

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: World-class striper fishery — Norris produces TN's largest stripers.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Clear deep TVA reservoir = trophy smallmouth habitat.

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Norris held the TN walleye record. Spring spawn run up the Powell + Clinch arms.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked by TWRA — rare in the Southeast.

patterns.title: Summer striper schooling
patterns.technique: Live shad on planer boards + downlines; topwater pencil poppers at dawn; casting Jigging Rapalas into surface boils
patterns.where: Main lake basin off Big Creek + Cove Creek; the river channels
patterns.details: Norris stripers gorge on summer threadfin shad. Schools blow up on the surface at dawn + dusk. Charter scene is robust.

patterns.title: Spring + fall smallmouth on rocky points
patterns.technique: Float-n-fly with hair jigs; Ned rigs; suspending jerkbaits
patterns.where: Rocky points + bluffs throughout the lake; spring transitions
patterns.details: Norris smallmouth are clear-water finicky. Float-n-fly with 1/8 oz hair jig is the Tennessee method. Cold-water months are best.

patterns.title: Walleye spring spawn run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow at 1/4-3/8 oz; small swimbaits; trolling Reef Runners
patterns.where: Powell + Clinch river arms; pre-spawn staging
patterns.details: Norris is the rare TN reservoir with serious walleye. Spring run up the river arms is the destination.

notes: East TN's premier deep-clear reservoir. 34,200 acres + 295 ft max depth. The most multi-species reservoir in TN.

name: Cherokee Lake

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Strong striper fishery. Summer thermal-refuge migration to Holston tailwater.

patterns.title: Striper trolling + planer boards
patterns.technique: Live shad on planer boards; downlines in summer thermocline; trolling umbrella rigs in spring
patterns.where: Main lake + the Holston River arm
patterns.details: Summer stripers chase the Holston tailwater cool water — see the Holston River entry for the fly fishery they create.

patterns.title: Spring crappie spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber
patterns.where: Brush + dock pilings in spawning coves

notes: Cherokee + Douglas + Boone are the Holston-system reservoirs. Cherokee is the largest of the three.

name: Douglas Lake

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Strong largemouth fishery — host of major bass tournaments.

species.name: White bass
species.notes: Spring run up the French Broad.

patterns.title: Spring white bass French Broad run
patterns.technique: Jigs + small swimbaits + in-line spinners
patterns.where: French Broad River arm above the lake
patterns.details: Massive spring schooling event. White bass push miles up the French Broad to spawn.

patterns.title: Spawn-season largemouth
patterns.technique: Senkos + lizards on beds; chatterbaits on pre-spawn flats
patterns.where: Flooded buckbrush + the upper-lake river channel arms

notes: Sevier County's big bass reservoir. Massive shoreline + heavy timber. Bass tournament destination.

name: Watauga Lake
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, walleye, lake trout, kokanee salmon

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Watauga Lake held the IGFA all-tackle walleye record at one point (25 lb in 1960).

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: One of the very few Southern lake-trout waters.

species.name: Kokanee salmon
species.notes: Stocked — rare Southern kokanee fishery.

patterns.title: Walleye deep summer trolling
patterns.technique: Lead-core or downrigger trolling; crawler harnesses; deep cranks
patterns.where: Main basin 30-80 ft
patterns.details: Watauga walleye orient to deep cool water in summer. The historical world record came from here.

patterns.title: Lake trout + kokanee deep jigging
patterns.technique: White tubes + small jigging spoons; jigging shad for kokanee
patterns.where: Deep basin 80-150 ft
patterns.details: One of very few Southern waters where you can target lake trout + kokanee. Unique fishery for TN.

managementProgram: TWRA walleye + lake trout + kokanee stocking
notes: High-elevation deep-clear reservoir. The closest thing to a Western lake in the South. Famous for the historical walleye world record.

name: South Holston Lake

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: Stocked.

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float; ultra-light line
patterns.where: Bluff banks + rocky points; cold-water suspended fish
patterns.details: The float-n-fly is a TN signature winter smallmouth technique. SoHo Lake is the home water for this method.

patterns.title: Walleye spring spawn run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow
patterns.where: Watauga River arm pre-spawn

notes: Sits above South Holston Dam — discharges feed the famous SoHo tailwater downstream.

name: Boone Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, smallmouth bass, crappie, walleye

patterns.title: Mixed bag spring crappie + bass
patterns.technique: Crappie jigs; spinnerbaits + plastic worms for bass
patterns.where: Coves + brush across the lake

notes: Tri-cities reservoir (Bristol / Johnson City / Kingsport). Multi-species generalist fishery.

name: Fort Loudoun Lake

patterns.title: Striper trolling downtown Knoxville
patterns.technique: Trolling planer boards; downlines in summer
patterns.where: Main channel through Knoxville

notes: The Knoxville-metro reservoir. Tennessee River through downtown.

name: Tellico Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, walleye, crappie, striped bass

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: TWRA stocked — strong populations.

patterns.title: Walleye on rocky points
patterns.technique: Crawler harnesses + jig + minnow
patterns.where: Main-lake points + river channel edges

notes: Adjacent to Fort Loudoun (they share a canal). Strong multi-species fishery.

name: Watts Bar Lake

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Fall/winter run up Clinch + Tennessee arms.

patterns.title: Striper trolling main channel
patterns.technique: Planer boards + downlines
patterns.where: Main Tennessee River channel

patterns.title: Sauger fall + winter
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow on current breaks
patterns.where: Below the Clinch + Emory confluences

notes: Confluence of the Tennessee + Clinch + Emory. Massive 39,000-acre reservoir.

name: Chickamauga Lake
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, striped bass, crappie, sauger

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Chickamauga produces the largest largemouths in TN — multiple state records. Florida-strain stocking + grass.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

patterns.title: Grass-mat largemouth (Chickamauga signature)
patterns.technique: Frog fishing over hydrilla + milfoil; punching heavy weights; chatterbaits on edges
patterns.where: Hydrilla flats throughout the lake — Sale Creek, Soddy-Daisy, Possum Creek
patterns.details: Chickamauga is THE Florida-strain largemouth destination in TN. The hydrilla beds produce 8-12 lb fish regularly. Bassmaster Elite + MLF tours visit. Frog bite is legendary.

patterns.title: Winter sauger run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow on current breaks
patterns.where: Below the Watts Bar Dam tailwater area

regulations: Largemouth slot limit 15-18 in (varies — confirm current TWRA regs).
notes: TN's premier trophy largemouth water. Two new TN state records have come from here in the past decade.

name: Nickajack Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, largemouth bass, sauger, crappie

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Below Chickamauga Dam tailwater.

patterns.title: Chickamauga Dam tailwater sauger
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow vertically
patterns.where: Immediately below Chickamauga Dam in the Nickajack headwaters

notes: Smaller link in the Tennessee River chain. Tailwater sauger fishery below Chickamauga Dam is the signature event.

name: Tims Ford Lake

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: One of the best smallmouth waters in Middle TN.

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float
patterns.where: Bluff banks + rocky points

notes: Feeds the Elk River tailwater. Clear deep reservoir, good multi-species.

name: Center Hill Lake

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: World-class — Center Hill stripers run with the largest in the South.

patterns.title: Summer striper schooling
patterns.technique: Live shad downlines + planer boards; topwater pencils at dawn
patterns.where: Main lake basin off Cookeville + Sligo

patterns.title: Float-n-fly smallmouth
patterns.technique: 1/8 oz hair jig under foam float
patterns.where: Bluff banks

notes: Sits above Center Hill Dam — discharges feed the Caney Fork tailwater. Premier striper + smallmouth combo.

name: Dale Hollow Lake

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: Dale Hollow holds the IGFA all-tackle world record smallmouth bass (11 lb 15 oz, 1955). Still produces 6+ lb fish.

species.name: Lake trout
species.notes: One of the rare Southern lake trout fisheries.

patterns.title: World-record smallmouth water
patterns.technique: Float-n-fly in cold months; jerkbaits + tubes + Ned in warmer months; live shiner traditional approach
patterns.where: Bluff walls + rocky points throughout the lake
patterns.details: Dale Hollow is the SMALLMOUTH lake — David Hayes' 11 lb 15 oz fish from 1955 still stands as the IGFA all-tackle record. Quality smallmouth fishing year-round.

notes: The smallmouth lake of America. Spans TN + KY. Clear deep TVA reservoir + native bass forage = world-class fish.

name: Old Hickory Lake
signatureSpecies: Striped bass, largemouth bass, crappie, white bass

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Below the dam in fall/winter.

patterns.title: Striper trolling Cumberland channel
patterns.technique: Planer boards + downlines with live shad
patterns.where: Main Cumberland River channel

patterns.title: Spring white bass run
patterns.technique: Jigs + spinners
patterns.where: Upper-lake tributary arms

notes: Nashville-metro reservoir on the Cumberland River. Most-fished by middle TN urban anglers.

name: J. Percy Priest Lake
signatureSpecies: Hybrid striped bass, largemouth bass, crappie

species.name: Hybrid striped bass
species.notes: Percy Priest is the premier hybrid striper water in TN.

patterns.title: Summer hybrid schooling
patterns.technique: Casting Jigging Rapalas + spoons into surface boils; pencil poppers at dawn
patterns.where: Main lake basin off Hamilton Creek + Stewarts Ferry
patterns.details: Percy Priest is the closest big-water reservoir to Nashville. Hybrid stripers school heavily in summer — birds working bait surface action.

notes: Nashville's home reservoir. 14,200 acres just SE of downtown. Hybrid striper destination.

name: Cordell Hull Lake
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, walleye, largemouth bass, striped bass

patterns.title: Smallmouth on rocky points
patterns.technique: Tubes + Ned rigs + jerkbaits
patterns.where: Main-lake points + bluff edges

notes: Cumberland River reservoir between Center Hill + Old Hickory. Underrated multi-species water.

name: Kentucky Lake (TN portion)

species.name: Crappie
species.notes: Kentucky Lake is the BEST crappie water in America by most measures. Tournaments + guide industry built around it.

species.name: Blue catfish
species.notes: World-class trophy blue cat fishery. Multiple state records.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

patterns.title: Spring crappie spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber; long-pole spider rigging
patterns.where: Brush + flooded timber in spawning coves; the Big Sandy + Eagle Creek arms
patterns.details: Kentucky Lake crappie spawn is one of the most-fished events in American freshwater. Spider-rigging with 8 rods is the local method. Limits of 1+ lb fish are routine.

patterns.title: Trophy blue catfish
patterns.technique: Cut shad / skipjack on big circle hooks; suspend or anchor in deep current breaks
patterns.where: Main river channel; below Kentucky Dam
patterns.details: Kentucky Lake produces 80+ lb blue cats every year. Cut bait on heavy gear. Charter scene is robust.

patterns.title: Winter sauger run
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow vertical
patterns.where: Below Pickwick Dam + the river channel

notes: America's premier crappie water. 110,000 acres on the TN side (the lake is huge, spanning TN + KY). Crappie + catfish + bass — everything.

name: Pickwick Lake (TN portion)
signatureSpecies: Smallmouth bass, largemouth bass, crappie, catfish

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.notes: One of the best trophy smallmouth waters in the South.

species.name: Sauger
species.notes: Winter run.

patterns.title: Pickwick smallmouth
patterns.technique: Jerkbaits, swimbaits, Ned rigs, drop-shot
patterns.where: Bluff walls + main-river channel points
patterns.details: Pickwick rivals Dale Hollow for trophy smallmouth in the South. Pickwick fish run shorter + heavier than Dale Hollow fish.

notes: Spans TN + MS + AL. The Tennessee River makes its big southward bend here. World-class smallmouth water.

name: Little River (Smokies)
signatureSpecies: Rainbow trout, brown trout, brook trout

species.name: Rainbow trout
species.notes: Wild + native-feel — these are stream-bred fish, not stocked.

species.name: Brown trout
species.notes: Wild population — bigger fish are downstream.

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Native specks above 3,000 ft elevation in headwaters.

species.name: Smallmouth bass
species.size: Lower river outside the park boundary

patterns.title: Yellow Stimulator + Adams dry-fly pocket water
patterns.technique: Size 12-16 Yellow Stimulator + size 14-18 Adams parachute; dry-dropper with small nymph below
patterns.where: Pocket water + plunge pools throughout the river
patterns.details: The Smokies dry-fly fishery is the most-accessible wild-trout fishing in the South. Yellow Stim is the universal searching dry. Short casts, drift the seams, hit every pocket. Fish are small but wild + plentiful.

patterns.title: Yallarhammer + Tellico Nymph wets
patterns.technique: Yallerhammer (size 12-14) + Tellico Nymph (size 12-14) swung downstream
patterns.where: Plunge pool tails + deeper runs
patterns.details: The Yallerhammer + Tellico Nymph are the regional wet-fly canon. Cherokee origin — fish like they've been fished here forever.

patterns.title: Headwater brook trout
patterns.technique: Small dries (#14-18); small streamers — small nymphs
patterns.where: Above 3,000 ft elevation; small headwater tributaries
patterns.details: Native Southern Appalachian brook trout — protected. Catch + release in most stretches. Fish are 5-9 in but breathtakingly colored.

regulations: Smokies single-hook + barbless requirements. Catch-and-release for native brook trout in many stretches. National Park fishing license + TN trout stamp.
notes: The most-fished Smokies stream. Townsend entrance to Great Smoky Mountains National Park. Wild + native-feel fishery.

name: Little Pigeon River — West Prong

species.name: Brook trout
species.notes: Headwaters.

patterns.title: Smokies dry-fly pocket water

patterns.where: Pocket water throughout

notes: Flows through Gatlinburg — busiest tourist gateway into the Smokies. Park stretch above town is the fishing water.

patterns.details: The Tellico Nymph was born on this river. It's a golden stonefly imitation that works year-round. The most distinctly TN fly pattern.

patterns.where: Cypress tree shadows + stump fields throughout the lake
patterns.details: Reelfoot bluegill are stupid + huge. The cypress-forest habitat is unique in the South. Use stealth.

regulations: Yo-yo rigs allowed for crappie (TN unique to Reelfoot). Bluegill management — generous limits but big fish are everywhere.
notes: TN's only natural lake. Formed by the 1812 New Madrid earthquake — submerged a hardwood forest, the cypress + stumps create unique habitat. Genuinely one-of-a-kind fishery.

name: Salmon Trout River
signatureSpecies: Coaster brook trout, brown trout, steelhead

species.name: Coaster brook trout
species.notes: The Salmon Trout is the LAST native coaster brook trout river in the lower 48. Critical conservation status.

patterns.title: Coaster brook trout — conservation status
patterns.technique: Small streamers (black-nose dace, sculpins); small spinners
patterns.where: Lower river up from Lake Superior
patterns.details: Coaster brook trout have nearly disappeared from Lake Superior. The Salmon Trout River + a few Canadian tribs are the last self-sustaining populations. CATCH + RELEASE. Use barbless. Keep them wet.

access: Limited public access; Huron Mountain Club controls much shoreline
regulations: Strict catch-and-release for coaster brook trout. Designated wild-trout stream.
managementProgram: MI DNR coaster brook trout conservation
notes: Coaster brook trout last stand. Access is heavily restricted because much of the watershed is private (Huron Mountain Club). One of the most important conservation fisheries in the Great Lakes basin.

patterns.details: The Mio-to-Foote stretch of the Au Sable is the trophy-brown alternative to the Holy Water. Bigger water, bigger fish, fewer fly anglers (more bait + lure pressure).

patterns.title: Hex hatch night fishing
patterns.technique: Size 6 Hex dries
patterns.where: Silt-bottom holes

notes: The "below Mio" stretch is technically still the Au Sable mainstem. Series of small dams (Mio, Alcona, Loud, Five Channels, Cooke, Foote) — wider + slower than the Holy Water.

name: Grand River — Upper (Lansing + above)

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: Below Lansing dams.

patterns.title: Lansing-stretch smallmouth + carp
patterns.technique: Tubes, Ned, in-line spinners; carp flies
patterns.where: Through downtown Lansing + East Lansing
patterns.details: Underrated urban smallmouth + carp through MSU's home city. Multiple low-head dams create habitat zones.

notes: Upper Grand. Lansing's urban smallmouth + carp fishery. Distinct from the salmon-zone Grand below Grand Rapids.

name: Lake Lansing

patterns.title: Suburban largemouth + bluegill
patterns.technique: Senkos for bass; wax worms for bluegill
patterns.where: Pad edges + dock corners

notes: East Lansing's home lake. MSU students fish here.

name: Reeds Lake

species.name: Walleye
species.notes: IDNR-stocked.

patterns.title: Deep-clear smallmouth
patterns.technique: Drop-shot, Ned
patterns.where: Deep humps + bluff edges

notes: East Grand Rapids' home lake. Small + deep + clear. Premier suburban GR fishery.

name: Crystal River + Kings Bay
signatureSpecies: Spotted sea trout, redfish, snook, sheepshead, tarpon

species.name: Spotted sea trout
species.notes: Kings Bay grass flats are a year-round trout factory. Bigger fish hold in winter when water cools.

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Tailing reds on shallow grass flats in calm low-tide windows. Bull reds offshore in fall.

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Northern range — sensitive to cold snaps. Spring-fed water gives them winter thermal refuge in Kings Bay.

species.name: Sheepshead
species.notes: Around dock pilings + oyster bars. Cold-water + spring fishery.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: May-July migration through. Less famous than Homosassa next door but quality fish.

species.name: Cobia
species.notes: Spring migration along Nature Coast.

species.name: Spanish mackerel
species.notes: Summer schools off the Crystal River nuclear plant warm-water discharge.

species.name: Florida pompano
species.notes: Beach surf + sandy flats.

patterns.title: Sight-fishing tailing redfish on Kings Bay grass flats
patterns.technique: Spoon Fly, Kwan, Borski Slider, small Merkin Crab on 8 wt with 16 lb fluoro; spin-cast 1/4 oz gold spoon, paddle-tail jerkbait
patterns.where: Shallow turtle-grass + sand-hole transitions throughout Kings Bay; tailing fish in <2 ft water on negative tides
patterns.details: The Citrus County signature fishery. Sight-fish from a skiff or kayak on calm low-tide mornings — wind kills it. Long leads ahead of the fish. The shallowest tails mean the most-spooky fish; downsize.

patterns.title: Grass-flat sea trout
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbaits (DOA, Z-Man), gold spoons, Cajun-thunder popping cork rigs; fly: Clouser, Surf Candy, Gurgler
patterns.where: 3-5 ft turtle-grass flats off Kings Bay; mid-bay flats off the Three Sisters area
patterns.details: Trout school heavily — when you find one, you find more. Winter cold-snap fishing under floats with shrimp produces the biggest sea trout.

patterns.title: Cold-snap snook in the spring runs
patterns.technique: Live shrimp on jigheads; Gartside Gurgler at low light; Schminnow + Bend-Back patterns
patterns.where: Three Sisters Springs vent + Kings Spring vent + the 72°F spring boils
patterns.details: During winter cold events, snook stack in the spring vents — water there stays a constant 72°F when surrounding flats drop into the 50s. Catch-and-release; respect the manatee zones (the snook are sharing thermal refuge with the manatees).

patterns.title: Tarpon migration through Crystal River
patterns.technique: Toad patterns, Cockroach, Apte II, EP Tarpon Streamer on 11-12 wt with 16-20 lb class tippet + 80 lb bite tippet
patterns.where: Outside the bar, the open Gulf shoreline, and the Crystal River channel entrance
patterns.details: Less crowded than Homosassa proper. Most boats stage out of Citrus County's public ramps. Watch tide windows + sun angle.

patterns.title: Sheepshead on dock pilings
patterns.technique: Live fiddler crab or shrimp on 1/0 hook + small split shot; cane-pole presentation against the pilings
patterns.where: Residential dock systems throughout Kings Bay
patterns.details: Sheepshead bite light — feel for weight, not a tap. Set the hook on any tension change.

regulations: No-motor + slow-speed zones for manatee protection. Snook closed in winter (cold-snap regs). FWC sportfishing license required. Citrus County sportfishing license valid statewide.
managementProgram: Crystal River National Wildlife Refuge — manatee protection
notes: Kings Bay is the heart of Crystal River — 72°F spring-fed bay draining the Gulf. Most famous as a manatee sanctuary, but underneath that is one of the best winter inshore fisheries in the South. Spring vents (Three Sisters, Kings Spring, Magnolia Spring) hold fish year-round. The Nature Coast moniker is real — almost no high-rise development, miles of grass flats.

name: Homosassa River + Homosassa Bay
signatureSpecies: Tarpon, redfish, spotted sea trout, snook

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: HOMOSASSA TARPON — legendary. Stu Apte + Lefty Kreh fished here. Considered THE giant-tarpon water in the world (along with Boca Grande). Massive May-June migration.

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Tailing reds on the back-bay flats.

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Like Crystal River — northern range, spring-vent thermal refuge in winter.

species.name: Cobia
species.notes: Spring migration through Nature Coast.

species.name: Spanish mackerel
species.notes: Summer schools.

patterns.title: Homosassa tarpon migration — the iconic flats fishery
patterns.when: May-June (peak), occasionally late April + early July
patterns.technique: Toad, Cockroach, Apte II, EP Tarpon Streamer, Black Death, Coker Smoker on 11-12 wt; live crab or threadfin for spin-tackle
patterns.where: Oklahoma + Black Rock + the channels into Homosassa Bay; the offshore migration corridor
patterns.details: The most-famous flats tarpon water on earth. Stu Apte called Homosassa the "tarpon capital." Big white sand-bottom flats with cruising lanes — sight-fish from a poled skiff. Crowded during peak weeks; the unwritten Homosassa etiquette matters. Hire a guide on your first trip.

patterns.title: Back-bay tailing redfish
patterns.technique: Spoon Fly, Kwan, small Merkin Crab on 8 wt; gold spoon + soft-plastic on spin
patterns.where: Back lakes + creek mouths off the main river; the Homosassa Bay flats at low tide
patterns.details: Same Citrus County playbook as Kings Bay but with more mangrove edge + creek-mouth structure.

patterns.title: Trout drift on grass flats
patterns.technique: Popping cork + soft-plastic jerkbait; gold spoon; fly with Clouser + Gurgler
patterns.where: Mid-bay grass flats outside the river mouth

patterns.title: Cold-snap snook at the spring runs
patterns.technique: Live shrimp + small jigheads; Gartside Gurgler at low light
patterns.where: Main spring vent (Homosassa Springs) + tributary spring vents
patterns.details: The river's headwater is Homosassa Springs (72°F year-round). Snook + tarpon + sea trout all use the spring as thermal refuge in cold events. Catch-and-release in the park-managed area.

regulations: FWC license. Tarpon regs — kill-tag required for harvest (rarely used; almost universally C+R).
notes: The most-storied flats-tarpon water in the world. The 1970s Homosassa scene with Stu Apte, Lefty Kreh, Tom Evans, Billy Pate produced the IGFA world records of the era + the modern saltwater fly canon. Still produces giants — 200+ pound fish caught annually.

name: Chassahowitzka River + Bay
signatureSpecies: Spotted sea trout, redfish, snook, largemouth bass

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Brackish-to-fresh transition above the spring run holds quality largemouth.

species.name: Bluegill + redear sunfish
species.notes: Above the salt line.

species.name: Tarpon
species.size: Smaller fish than Homosassa — 30-80 lb migrating juveniles.

patterns.title: Brackish-zone redfish + trout
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbait, Spoon Fly, gold spoon
patterns.where: Creek mouths + grass flats outside the river

patterns.title: Spring-run largemouth + bream
patterns.technique: Topwater frog, senko; cricket + bobber for bream
patterns.where: Above Crawford Creek + Salt Creek confluences — fresh-water zone
patterns.details: Chass has the rare brackish-fresh transition that gives anglers a multi-species day from one ramp.

notes: The "Chazz" — smaller + quieter than Homosassa. Springs-fed, narrow, character-filled. Wild Florida feel.

name: Halls River (Homosassa tributary)
signatureSpecies: Redfish, sea trout, snook, largemouth bass

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Spring-vent winter refuge.

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Brackish-fresh transition.

patterns.title: Creek-mouth redfish
patterns.technique: Gold spoon, soft-plastic jerkbait, Kwan fly
patterns.where: Mouth of Halls River at Homosassa River confluence

notes: Major Homosassa River tributary. Quieter side-water fishery.

name: Ozello / Salt River back-lake system

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Ozello's maze of back lakes + creeks is one of the best redfish destinations in FL.

patterns.title: Maze-of-back-lakes redfish
patterns.technique: Spoon Fly, Kwan, Borski Slider; gold spoon, paddle-tail jerkbait
patterns.where: Network of unmarked back lakes + creeks between Ozello + Crystal Bay; tailing fish on flats
patterns.details: Ozello = "the maze" — narrow channels through mangrove islands open into small lakes. Local knowledge or a guide is mandatory; GPS waypoints rule. Quiet electric-motor work, no big-engine running.

patterns.title: Winter trout on the deeper Salt River channels
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbait + popping cork; live shrimp on jigheads
patterns.where: Deeper holes in the Salt River main channel

notes: Citrus County's "secret" inshore fishery. Local Ozello guides specialize in the maze. Pristine — no development, no boat traffic outside guide skiffs.

name: Rainbow River + Rainbow Springs
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, bluegill, redear sunfish (shellcracker), spotted sunfish, mullet

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Crystal-clear spring-fed water; sight-fishing in 6-15 ft.

species.name: Redear sunfish (shellcracker)
species.notes: Rainbow River shellcrackers are renowned — clear water + abundant snail forage = big bream.

species.name: Mullet
species.notes: Schooled mullet visible in the clear water; not really a fly target but they're everywhere.

patterns.title: Sight-fishing crystal-water bass
patterns.technique: Lightly-weighted senko, Texas-rigged worm, finesse jig; fly: small streamers, sliders
patterns.where: Spring boils + downstream pools; visible cruising fish
patterns.details: The water is so clear (visibility 30+ ft) that you sight-fish bass like trout. Long leaders, light tippet, finesse presentations.

patterns.title: Trophy shellcracker on snail beds
patterns.technique: Worm or cricket on small hook + bobber; 1/64-1/32 oz jig
patterns.where: Sandy bottom + spawning beds visible from the surface

regulations: No internal-combustion engines in upper reaches. Electric-only on the upper Rainbow.
notes: Technically headwaters in Marion County (Rainbow Springs), flows through Citrus County to the Withlacoochee. One of the clearest rivers in North America — 6+ million gallons of spring water per hour. Tubing destination but also a serious fishery if you go before/after summer.

name: Withlacoochee River (Citrus + Hernando)
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, bluegill, redear sunfish, channel catfish

patterns.title: Bass + bream from a paddle craft
patterns.technique: Senko, topwater frog, popper for bass; cricket + bobber for bream
patterns.where: Cypress + tupelo edges; oxbow swamps

patterns.title: Speck (crappie) winter schools
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under bobber
patterns.where: Deep holes + brush above Lake Rousseau

notes: Wild Florida river through cypress swamps. Joins Lake Rousseau then exits to the Gulf at Yankeetown. Quiet paddle-fishing river — wildlife-rich.

name: Lake Rousseau
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, black crappie, bluegill, channel catfish

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Lake Rousseau is the best bass lake in west-central FL outside the Kissimmee chain.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Stocked — landlocked striper fishery.

patterns.title: Pad + cypress largemouth
patterns.technique: Topwater frog, weighted Senko, swim jig, flipping plastic
patterns.where: Pad fields + cypress stand-offs throughout the impoundment
patterns.details: Flooded cypress + lily pads + Kissimmee grass = classic Florida bass habitat. Punch the mats in summer.

patterns.title: Speck spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under cork; spider rigging multi-pole
patterns.where: Cypress stumps + brush in 5-8 ft

notes: Withlacoochee River impoundment created for the Cross-Florida Barge Canal (never completed). Flooded cypress forest + endless pad fields = bass-fishing heaven.

name: Tsala Apopka Lake Chain
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, black crappie, bluegill, redear sunfish

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Tsala Apopka grows Florida-strain bass. Multiple lake pools = different fishing characters.

patterns.title: Pad-and-pencil-reed largemouth (the FL chain-lake game)
patterns.technique: Hollow-body frog over pads; weighted senko in pockets; swim jig + chatterbait on outside grass edges; wild-shiner under cork for trophy class
patterns.where: Pad fields + pencil reeds + Kissimmee grass + cypress points across all three pools
patterns.details: Three connected pools — Inverness Pool (north), Floral City Pool (south), Hernando Pool (middle). Each fishes a little different. Inverness has more grass; Floral City has more pads. Wild shiners catch the trophy fish.

patterns.title: Spawn shellcracker on snail beds
patterns.technique: Cricket or worm on 1/64 oz jig + bobber; tipped with small live shiner for big bream
patterns.where: Sand-bottom spawn pockets visible from the surface; 2-4 ft
patterns.details: Tsala Apopka shellcrackers run big — 10-12 in fish common in good years.

patterns.title: Speck spawn under cypress
patterns.technique: Crappie jig + minnow under cork; spider rigging
patterns.where: Flooded cypress + brush in 5-8 ft

regulations: FL bass slot + 5-fish limit. Specks 25/day no size limit.
notes: The biggest natural-lake system in Citrus County. Three interconnected pools spanning ~22,000 acres. Old Florida bass + bream destination — 70+ year history. Wild Florida feel, low-rise shoreline, lots of cypress.

name: Lake Henderson (Inverness)

patterns.title: Suburban Inverness bass fishery
patterns.technique: Senko, frog, swim jig, wild-shiner under cork
patterns.where: Dock corners + pad edges around the city of Inverness

notes: Connected to the Tsala Apopka chain via channel. Inverness's home lake — fishable urban bass + crappie.

name: Nature Coast Gulf Flats (Citrus Co. shoreline)
signatureSpecies: Spotted sea trout, redfish, cobia, Spanish mackerel, grouper

species.name: Cobia
species.notes: Nature Coast cobia migration is March-May — sight-fishing along the inshore turtle-grass shoreline.

species.name: Spanish mackerel
species.notes: Summer schools.

species.name: King mackerel
species.notes: Spring + fall runs offshore.

species.name: Grouper (gag + red)
species.notes: Offshore Nature Coast ledges.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: May-June migration along the shoreline.

species.name: Florida pompano
species.notes: Sandy flats + beach surf.

patterns.title: Sight-fishing cobia on the inshore shoreline
patterns.technique: Big bucktail jig (3-4 oz, chartreuse); live pinfish; fly: Cobia Tube Fly + big bucktail-style streamers
patterns.where: Inside the 4-7 ft turtle-grass shoreline; sight-fishing single fish + pairs cruising
patterns.details: The Nature Coast spring cobia migration is one of FL's best sight-fisheries. Tower boats run the shoreline scanning. Captain has to spot the fish + cast quickly — they don't wait around.

patterns.title: Mid-bay sea trout drift
patterns.technique: Popping cork + soft-plastic jerkbait; gold spoon; fly: Clouser, Gurgler
patterns.where: Mid-bay 4-7 ft grass flats

patterns.title: Offshore grouper bottom-fishing
patterns.technique: Live pinfish on circle hooks + heavy sinker; jigs
patterns.where: Limestone ledges 30-100 ft offshore from Crystal River + Homosassa

regulations: FWC saltwater license. Cobia 36" min, 1/day. Sea trout slot (varies by zone). Snook closed in cold. Grouper seasons.
notes: The shoreline strip from Crystal River through Yankeetown is the famed "Nature Coast" — undeveloped, low-rise, miles of turtle-grass flats. Inshore + offshore mixed-bag waters; charter scene runs out of Crystal River, Homosassa, Ozello, Yankeetown.

name: Lake Okeechobee (the Big O)
county: Glades / Hendry / Martin / Okeechobee / Palm Beach
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, black crappie, bluegill, redear sunfish, channel catfish

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: America's bass lake. Florida-strain bass + endless habitat. Multiple FLW + Bassmaster Elite tournaments here annually.

species.name: Black crappie (specks)
species.notes: Spring speck spawn is one of the most-fished events in FL freshwater.

patterns.title: Punching the mats (the Big O bass signature)
patterns.technique: Heavy 1-1.5 oz tungsten + creature bait (Sweet Beaver, Brush Hog) on heavy braid; flippin' stick rod
patterns.where: Hyacinth + hydrilla mats; "punch holes" through dense vegetation
patterns.details: Tournaments are won on the punch bite. Florida-strain bass live UNDER the mats in summer + cold. Heavy gear non-negotiable — 65 lb braid + heavy hook.

patterns.title: Wild-shiner trophy bass
patterns.technique: Wild golden shiners under a float on heavy spinning gear
patterns.where: Outside grass edges + reed clumps + hyacinth lines
patterns.details: The trophy way. Wild shiners (8-10 in) catch the 10+ lb fish that won't commit to artificials. Guide-led shiner trips out of Clewiston + Belle Glade + Okeechobee City are the established trophy program.

patterns.title: Open-water speck spawn
patterns.technique: Spider-rigging jig + minnow on multi-pole rigs; pitch jigs to brush + holes
patterns.where: Open-water brush piles; the rim canal

patterns.title: Bream + shellcracker shoreline
patterns.technique: Cricket + bobber; small popper for bluegill on fly rod
patterns.where: Shoreline + Kissimmee River mouth + outflow canals

regulations: FL freshwater license. 5-bass limit; only 1 over 16 in. Slot rules vary — check current FWC regs.
managementProgram: Army Corps of Engineers lake-level management
notes: The Big O. 730 square miles + 448,000 acres = the second-largest freshwater lake entirely in the contiguous US (only Lake Michigan is bigger). Average depth 9 ft, max 12 ft — shallow, weedy, hot. Hurricanes drive water-quality cycles. Trophy bass capital of the world.

name: Lake Tohopekaliga (Lake Toho)

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Kissimmee-chain Florida-strain bass. Pat Cullen's 12-pounder set a Toho record. Major tournament destination.

patterns.title: Pre-spawn flipping + topwater
patterns.technique: Flipping jig + creature bait into pencil reeds; weighted senko; topwater walking bait (Spook + Sammy) at dawn
patterns.where: Pencil reeds + bulrush + hydrilla edges; the spawn flats off Goblets Cove + Big Grassy
patterns.details: Toho pre-spawn produces the biggest fish of the year. Major bass tournaments time around this window.

patterns.title: Wild-shiner trophy season
patterns.technique: Wild shiners under cork on heavy spinning gear
patterns.where: Outside grass edges, "scattered" hydrilla patches

patterns.title: Speck winter night fishing
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow over brush piles; nighttime lights drawing baitfish + crappie
patterns.where: Bridge lights + nighttime brush stops

regulations: FL bass slot limit; TrophyCatch eligible.
notes: Lake "Toho" — anchor of the Kissimmee chain. Trophy bass destination, multiple Bassmaster Classics. Connected to East Toho + downstream to Cypress + Hatchineha + Kissimmee + Okeechobee via the Kissimmee River system.

name: East Lake Tohopekaliga

patterns.title: Florida-strain bass on grass edges
patterns.technique: Senko, weighted plastic, swim jig, chatterbait
patterns.where: Hydrilla + Kissimmee grass + pencil reed edges

notes: East Toho — quieter sister to the main Toho. Same Florida-strain bass fishery without the tournament traffic.

name: Lake Kissimmee

patterns.title: Wild-shiner trophy bass on pencil reeds
patterns.technique: Wild shiner under cork
patterns.where: Pencil reed outside edges + scattered hydrilla
patterns.details: The trophy program. Local guides out of Camp Mack work the Kissimmee for 10+ lb fish.

patterns.title: Mat-punching summer
patterns.technique: Heavy tungsten + creature bait punching hyacinth mats
patterns.where: Mat lines across the lake

notes: Third lake in the Kissimmee Chain. Wilder + less developed than Toho. Multiple state-record bass have come from the Kissimmee chain over the decades.

name: Lake Hatchineha + Lake Cypress

patterns.title: Pad-edge + cypress bass
patterns.technique: Frog, senko, swim jig, wild shiner under cork
patterns.where: Cypress points + pad edges

notes: Two connected lakes mid-Kissimmee chain. Bass-tournament + trophy bream water. Less famous than Toho/Kissimmee = less pressure.

name: Harris Chain of Lakes
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, black crappie, bluegill, redear sunfish

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Lake Apopka was once notoriously polluted; restoration brought it back. Lake Yale + Eustis + Harris hold the trophy fish.

species.name: Black crappie
species.notes: Spring spawn is the heaviest-fished crappie event in central FL.

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Stocked into select lakes.

patterns.title: Pre-spawn bass on the spawn flats
patterns.technique: Lipless crank, chatterbait, swim jig, weighted senko
patterns.where: Sand-bottom spawn flats off Lake Yale + Eustis

patterns.title: Trophy speck spawn
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow under cork; spider rigging
patterns.where: Lake Griffin + Lake Yale brush piles; the canal connectors
patterns.details: Lake Griffin is the celebrity speck water of the chain. Limits of 13-inchers are routine in peak weeks.

notes: Seven connected lakes northwest of Orlando: Harris, Eustis, Dora, Beauclair, Apopka, Yale, Griffin. Connected by channels + locks. ~76,000 acres of bass + speck water. Apopka was the most-polluted; cleanup over the past 20 years restored its bass fishery.

name: Lake George (St. Johns River)
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, striped bass, black crappie, channel catfish

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Atlantic-strain stripers run up the St. Johns; Lake George is staging water.

patterns.title: St. Johns River striper run
patterns.technique: Live shad + threadfin on planer boards; trolling crankbaits + umbrella rigs
patterns.where: Lake George main basin + the St. Johns River channel entering + exiting the lake

patterns.title: Lake George bass
patterns.technique: Senko, frog, swim jig, wild shiner
patterns.where: Pad fields + grass edges along the lake shoreline

managementProgram: St. Johns River Water Management District
notes: Second-largest natural lake in FL (after Okeechobee). 12 mi long, 6 mi wide, all 12 ft deep. The St. Johns River flows through it. Striper + bass combo is rare for FL.

name: Stick Marsh / Farm 13 (Headwaters)

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: World-famous trophy bass water. C+R only. The Farm 13 record bass is genuinely legendary.

patterns.title: Flipping the flooded timber
patterns.technique: Heavy creature bait flipped into timber; wild shiner on heavy gear
patterns.where: Flooded timber + standing dead trees across the reservoir
patterns.details: Stick Marsh produces 10+ lb largemouth at a frequency unmatched in FL. C+R only — every fish goes back. Tournaments are barbless + paper-only.

access: Stick Marsh Boat Ramp (Florida Game Commission)
regulations: CATCH AND RELEASE ONLY for largemouth. All bass must be released alive.
managementProgram: St. Johns River Water Management District
notes: AKA "Farm 13/Stick Marsh" — flooded agricultural land reclaimed as the headwaters of the St. Johns River. The catch-and-release rule + flooded-timber habitat produces a density of giants found almost nowhere else. Iconic FL bass-fishing destination.

name: Rodman Reservoir (Lake Ocklawaha)

patterns.title: Flooded-timber bass
patterns.technique: Frog over slop, weighted senko in pockets, flipping creature into timber
patterns.where: Standing timber + grass mats

notes: Cross-Florida Barge Canal impoundment of the Ocklawaha River. Periodic drawdowns concentrate fish. Bass + bream destination.

name: Lake Talquin
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, black crappie, striped bass, bluegill

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Stocked.

patterns.title: Crappie + bass combo trip
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow for crappie; swim jig + senko for bass
patterns.where: Standing timber + creek arms

notes: Ochlockonee River impoundment west of Tallahassee. Standing flooded timber + creek arms. Panhandle bass + crappie destination.

name: St. Johns River (Florida)
signatureSpecies: Largemouth bass, striped bass, black crappie, channel catfish, blue catfish

species.name: Striped bass
species.notes: Atlantic-strain spring run from Jacksonville into the upper St. Johns.

species.name: Blue catfish
species.notes: Increasing population in the lower river.

species.name: Sunshine bass (hybrid striper × white bass)
species.notes: Stocked into the upper river.

patterns.title: Spring striper run
patterns.technique: Live shad on planer boards; trolling crankbaits
patterns.where: River channel from Jacksonville up through Welaka, Astor, Lake George

patterns.title: Pad-and-eelgrass largemouth
patterns.technique: Frog, senko, flipping jig
patterns.where: Pad edges + cypress in the middle + upper river

managementProgram: St. Johns River Water Management District
notes: Florida's longest river (310 miles), one of the few that flows NORTH. Drains to Jacksonville. Massive multi-species river — bass + striper + catfish + bream. Manatees common.

name: Mosquito Lagoon
signatureSpecies: Redfish, spotted sea trout, black drum

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Mosquito Lagoon is the gold-standard sight-fishing flats for redfish in the eastern US. Crystal-clear water + abundant grass + cruising tailing fish.

species.name: Spotted sea trout
species.notes: Trophy gator-trout water. The Lagoon's flats give up 8-pound trout regularly.

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Northern range; thermal-refuge issue in cold.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Migrating fish summer.

patterns.title: Tailing redfish + gator trout on grass flats
patterns.technique: Spoon Fly, Kwan, Borski Slider on 8 wt with 16 lb fluoro; gold spoon + paddle-tail jerkbait + DOA on spin gear
patterns.where: Shallow turtle-grass flats with sand-hole transitions; the iconic "Indian River Lagoon style" 1-3 ft tailing water
patterns.details: Mosquito Lagoon is THE redfish flats — the place tournament + magazine guides go. Best on calm low-tide mornings. Long leads, downsized flies, full stealth.

patterns.title: Gator trout on potholes
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbait + popping cork; fly: Clouser + Surf Candy
patterns.where: Sand potholes within the turtle-grass flats
patterns.details: Trophy trout (28+ in, 8+ lb) hold in the potholes when water cools.

regulations: FWC saltwater license. Redfish + trout slot limits (vary).
notes: World-class shallow-flats sight-fishery. Adjacent to Kennedy Space Center — the protected status keeps development out + the fishery pristine.

name: Indian River Lagoon
county: Brevard / Indian River / St. Lucie / Martin
signatureSpecies: Redfish, spotted sea trout, snook, tarpon

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Spillways + inlets are snook factories.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Migration corridor.

patterns.title: Spillway snook
patterns.technique: Hot Lips foam shrimp, paddle-tail jerkbait, live shrimp under cork
patterns.where: C-44 + S-80 + other freshwater discharge spillways from Lake Okeechobee + St. Johns
patterns.details: Snook stack at spillway outflows when freshwater is being released. Predictable + technique-driven fishery.

patterns.title: Sea trout drift
patterns.technique: Popping cork + soft plastic; gold spoon
patterns.where: Mid-lagoon grass flats

notes: The longer system south of Mosquito Lagoon. 156-mile estuary, the most biologically diverse estuary in North America. Multiple inlets connect to the Atlantic.

name: Banana River Lagoon

patterns.title: No-motor-zone tailing reds
patterns.technique: Spoon Fly, Kwan; kayak / paddleboard / electric-only
patterns.where: NASA No-Motor Zone flats (the back of the Banana River)
patterns.details: The back of the Banana River is a No-Motor Zone — kayak + electric only. That keeps the pressure low + the fish unspooked. Genuinely one of FL's best kayak-fly destinations.

regulations: No-Motor Zone in the back of the Banana River. Kayak / electric / paddle only.
notes: Adjacent to Kennedy Space Center / Cape Canaveral. The NMZ creates a pristine kayak-only fishery.

name: Sebastian Inlet
signatureSpecies: Snook, tarpon, redfish, Spanish mackerel, jacks

patterns.title: Inlet snook on the rocks
patterns.technique: Bucktail jig + Gulp shrimp; large soft-plastic jerkbait; live mullet
patterns.where: Bridge pilings + jetty rocks
patterns.details: Sebastian Inlet is one of the most-fished snook spots in FL. Catwalk + jetty access. Strong currents + structure.

notes: Premier shore-access saltwater fishery on the central east coast. Big fish + heavy current.

name: Stuart / St. Lucie River

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Stuart is a major migration corridor + summer staging area.

species.name: Sailfish
species.notes: Stuart = sailfish capital of the world (offshore).

species.name: King mackerel
species.notes: Offshore.

species.name: Mahi (dolphin)
species.notes: Offshore.

patterns.title: Stuart sailfish offshore
patterns.technique: Trolling rigged ballyhoo + dredges; fly-fishing teasers with Cam Sigler tube fly
patterns.where: Gulf Stream off Stuart (8-30 mi offshore)
patterns.details: Stuart bills itself as the sailfish capital of the world. Tournament season is January. Charter scene out of Stuart + Hutchinson Island.

patterns.title: St. Lucie inshore snook + tarpon
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbait, Gartside Gurgler, live mullet
patterns.where: St. Lucie River channel + Roosevelt Bridge + inlet

notes: Stuart = "Sailfish Capital." Offshore: sailfish, mahi, king mackerel, tuna. Inshore: snook, tarpon, redfish, trout. Multi-species hub.

name: Loxahatchee River (Jupiter)

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: The Loxahatchee is famous for JUVENILE tarpon — the back-river canals + pools hold 5-20 lb baby tarpon year-round. Great learning water.

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Upper river fresh-water reach.

patterns.title: Juvenile tarpon in back-river canals
patterns.technique: Small tarpon flies (Cockroach, Toad) on 7-9 wt; small plugs + DOA shrimp on light spin
patterns.where: Back-river canals + pools above Jupiter Inlet
patterns.details: The Loxahatchee's juvenile tarpon are a learning-water gift. Smaller flies, lighter tackle, more catches.

notes: Designated National Wild + Scenic River. Unique cypress + mangrove transition.

name: St. Johns River (Jacksonville / Mayport)
signatureSpecies: Redfish, spotted sea trout, flounder, sheepshead, tarpon

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Jetty + ICW + flats redfish through Jacksonville.

species.name: Flounder
species.notes: Jacksonville is the southern flounder destination on the east coast.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Spring + summer migration through the river mouth.

species.name: Largemouth bass
species.notes: Upriver freshwater.

patterns.title: Mayport jetty redfish
patterns.technique: Bucktail jig + Gulp; live shrimp; soft-plastic paddle tail
patterns.where: Mayport jetty + the river mouth

patterns.title: Flounder on the bottom
patterns.technique: Jig + minnow on bottom; live mud minnow or finger mullet
patterns.where: ICW channels + ledges; oyster bars

notes: St. Johns River mouth. North FL inshore + nearshore mixed-bag.

name: St. Augustine + Matanzas River

species.name: Flounder
species.notes: Fall run.

patterns.title: Oyster-bar redfish
patterns.technique: Gold spoon, soft-plastic, Spoon Fly
patterns.where: Oyster bars + creek mouths along the Matanzas

notes: Historic St. Augustine area. Quiet inshore fishery with oyster bars + grass flats. Less pressure than Mosquito Lagoon.

name: Tampa Bay
signatureSpecies: Spotted sea trout, redfish, snook, tarpon, mackerel

species.name: Spotted sea trout
species.notes: Tampa Bay trout population is exceptional — the bay's grass flats are productive year-round.

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Year-round resident + spawn-season at the passes.

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: May-July migration through Tampa Bay; pre-Boca-Grande staging.

species.name: Permit
species.size: Smaller juveniles on flats; offshore wrecks for bigger fish

patterns.title: Spring + summer tarpon at the passes
patterns.technique: Live crab on circle hook; live pinfish; fly: Toad, Cockroach, Apte II
patterns.where: Egmont Channel + the passes (Egmont Key area, Skyway shipping channel) + bay flats
patterns.details: Tampa Bay tarpon stage in deeper bay channels + the passes before pushing to Boca Grande. Pre-spawn fish are aggressive.

patterns.title: Snook spawn at the passes
patterns.technique: Live pinfish + sardine; pearl/chartreuse soft-plastic; fly: Schminnow, Bend-Back
patterns.where: Pass-a-Grille, Egmont Channel, Bunces Pass

patterns.title: Mid-bay grass flats trout
patterns.technique: Popping cork + soft-plastic; gold spoon; fly: Clouser, Gurgler
patterns.where: Old Tampa Bay, Lower Tampa Bay, Cockroach Bay grass flats

notes: Florida's largest open-water estuary. 400,000 acres of grass flats + passes + mangrove. Year-round multi-species + tarpon highlight reel.

name: Boca Grande Pass + Charlotte Harbor
signatureSpecies: Tarpon, snook, redfish, spotted sea trout

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: BOCA GRANDE = the tarpon capital of the world by sheer numbers. Mid-May through July, thousands of fish stack in Boca Grande Pass.

species.name: Goliath grouper
species.notes: C+R only — protected.

patterns.title: Boca Grande Pass tarpon — the iconic event
patterns.technique: Live crab on circle hook; live pass crab; pinfish; fly fishing in the back-bay edges (the pass itself is too deep for fly comfortably)
patterns.where: Boca Grande Pass (mouth) and the channels into Charlotte Harbor
patterns.details: The most-fished tarpon event in the world. Crowds of 100+ boats some days. Local etiquette + tournament rules apply. Hire a guide.

patterns.title: Charlotte Harbor inshore mixed-bag
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic jerkbait, gold spoon, live shrimp, fly with Clouser + Gurgler
patterns.where: Mangrove shoreline + oyster bars + grass flats throughout the harbor

notes: Charlotte Harbor is Florida's second-largest open-water estuary. Boca Grande Pass is the famous mouth. Mid-summer tarpon migration is the world's most-fished tarpon event.

name: Pine Island Sound + Sanibel / Captiva
signatureSpecies: Redfish, spotted sea trout, snook, tarpon

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Boca Grande Pass nearby.

species.name: Permit
species.notes: Beach permit cruising — sight-fishery.

patterns.title: Sanibel beach snook (Schminnow water)
patterns.technique: Schminnow, Crystal Schminnow, Floating Minnow; small white DOA shrimp
patterns.where: Sanibel + Captiva beach shoreline
patterns.details: Norm Zeigler's Schminnow was invented here — Sanibel beach snook eat it without question. Wade or kayak the surf.

patterns.title: Mangrove-shoreline redfish
patterns.technique: Gold spoon, weedless jerkbait
patterns.where: Mangrove shoreline + oyster bars in Pine Island Sound

notes: Sanibel + Captiva are the famous islands; Pine Island Sound separates them from the mainland. Premier multi-species flats.

name: Naples / Estero Bay

species.name: Snook
species.notes: Naples is one of the best year-round snook fisheries in FL.

patterns.title: Mangrove tunnel snook
patterns.technique: Weedless jerkbait + Bend-Back fly into mangrove tunnels
patterns.where: Estero Bay mangroves + Naples back-bay residential canals

notes: SW FL's premier snook + redfish flats. Quieter than the Keys or Tampa Bay.

name: 10,000 Islands + Everglades National Park backcountry
signatureSpecies: Snook, redfish, tarpon, snapper, juvenile tarpon

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Everglades = the iconic tarpon backcountry. Big migrations + juvenile fish year-round.

species.name: Goliath grouper
species.notes: C+R protected.

patterns.title: Mangrove-tunnel snook
patterns.technique: Bend-Back, Schminnow, weedless plastic; live pinfish
patterns.where: Mangrove root systems + creek mouths
patterns.details: The Everglades backcountry is a maze of mangrove islands + creeks. Local knowledge or a guide is mandatory — GPS waypoints rule.

patterns.title: Backcountry tarpon
patterns.technique: Toad, Cockroach, Tarpon Bunny on 11 wt; live mullet
patterns.where: Backcountry channels + bays leading from Florida Bay to the Gulf

regulations: Everglades National Park rules + permits in park-managed water.
notes: 1.5 million acres of mangrove maze. The iconic Florida tarpon backcountry — Hemingway, Zane Grey, every generation of saltwater fly anglers. Guided trip strongly recommended for first-timers.

name: Biscayne Bay

species.name: Bonefish
species.notes: Biscayne Bay bonefishing is the urban-flats classic — sight-fishing skinny water in view of downtown Miami.

species.name: Sailfish
species.notes: Offshore.

patterns.title: Biscayne Bay bonefish flats
patterns.technique: Crazy Charlie, Gotcha, Kwan, Bonefish Bitters; small spin-cast jigs
patterns.where: Bay flats from Stiltsville to the Keys + Card Sound + Ragged Keys
patterns.details: Biscayne Bay bones are a sight-fishery — wading + poling from skiffs. Permits, tarpon, snook share the same water.

patterns.title: Stiltsville permit
patterns.technique: Merkin Crab, EP Crab, small soft-plastic shrimp
patterns.where: Stiltsville flats + Biscayne National Park flats

notes: Half urban (downtown Miami shoreline), half Biscayne National Park. The Kwan fly was developed here.

name: Florida Bay (Flamingo + south Everglades)
signatureSpecies: Tarpon, redfish, snook, bonefish, sea trout

species.name: Bonefish
species.notes: Smaller pop than the Keys proper.

patterns.title: Flamingo backcountry tarpon + snook
patterns.technique: Tarpon flies + small streamers + soft-plastic; live mullet
patterns.where: Whitewater Bay + Cape Sable + the back-bay creek mouths

notes: Massive shallow flat-bottomed bay between the Keys + the Everglades. The "backcountry" — opposite of Atlantic-side glamour.

name: Islamorada Flats (Upper Keys)

species.name: Bonefish
species.notes: Islamorada = "sportfishing capital of the world" — the famous Florida Keys flats epicenter.

species.name: Redfish
species.notes: Backcountry side toward Florida Bay.

species.name: Sailfish + mahi
species.notes: Offshore.

patterns.title: Bonefish flats (the Keys classic)
patterns.technique: Crazy Charlie, Gotcha, Kwan, Bonefish Bitters on 8 wt
patterns.where: Ocean-side + bay-side flats throughout the Upper Keys
patterns.details: The Keys bonefish are notoriously spooky + selective. Long leaders, light tippet, perfect presentations.

patterns.title: Permit on the flats
patterns.technique: Merkin Crab, EP Crab, Avalon Permit Fly
patterns.where: Bay-side flats + tarpon highway near-shore

patterns.title: Backcountry tarpon (Florida Bay side)
patterns.technique: Toad, Cockroach, Apte II
patterns.where: Florida Bay backcountry to the north of Islamorada

notes: Islamorada — the most-fished flats town in the world. Permit + bonefish + tarpon flats program built around hundreds of guides.

name: Marathon Flats (Middle Keys)

patterns.title: Middle Keys flats
patterns.technique: Same Keys playbook — bonefish flies + permit crabs + tarpon flies
patterns.where: Bay-side + ocean-side flats

notes: Quieter alternative to Islamorada. Same flats fishery character.

name: Lower Keys (Big Pine / No Name Key)

patterns.title: Big Pine Key flats
patterns.technique: Permit crabs + bonefish flies
patterns.where: Niles Channel + Big Pine flats

notes: The quietest of the Keys flats systems. Local guides specialize here.

name: Key West Backcountry + Marquesas

species.name: Lemon shark
species.notes: Sight-fishing on the flats — a fly-rod adventure target.

patterns.title: Marquesas permit
patterns.technique: Merkin Crab, EP Crab, Avalon Permit Fly on 10 wt
patterns.where: Marquesas Atoll flats (30 mi west of Key West)
patterns.details: Marquesas is the legendary permit destination. Boat run from Key West + flats wading on uninhabited islands.

patterns.title: Lemon shark sight-fishing
patterns.technique: Big Red Shark Fly + chum
patterns.where: Backcountry flats around Boca Grande Key + Marquesas

access: Key West (Stock Island, Geiger Key, Garrison Bight)
notes: Key West = end of the road but middle of the action. Marquesas = the legendary permit + tarpon destination. Dry Tortugas farther west still.

name: Dry Tortugas

species.name: Mutton snapper

patterns.title: Mutton snapper bottom-fishing
patterns.technique: Live pinfish + ballyhoo on circle hooks; jig in 80-200 ft
patterns.where: Dry Tortugas reefs + wrecks

access: Boat-in only from Key West (~70 mi west)
notes: 70 miles west of Key West. Multi-day boat trips. Mutton snapper + permit + grouper destination. Fort Jefferson is the historic landmark.

name: Apalachicola Bay

species.name: Tripletail

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Summer migration.

patterns.title: Tripletail sight-fishing under buoys
patterns.technique: Live shrimp, soft-plastic jig, fly: Schminnow + small Clouser
patterns.where: Crab-pot buoys + floating debris in the bay + nearshore
patterns.details: Tripletail hold under structure on the surface. Cruise crab-pot lines + bait the fish with shrimp. A genuinely unique FL sight-fishery.

patterns.title: Grass-flat trout
patterns.technique: Soft-plastic + popping cork; gold spoon
patterns.where: East Bay + West Bay grass flats

notes: Famous oyster bay (recovering). Inshore + nearshore Panhandle classic.

name: St. Joseph Bay (Port St. Joe)

species.name: Spotted sea trout

species.name: Pompano

species.name: Tarpon
species.notes: Summer migration.

patterns.where: St. Joe Bay grass flats + sand-hole transitions

notes: Pristine Panhandle bay. Famous scallop fishery + trophy trout flats.

name: Choctawhatchee Bay (Destin)
signatureSpecies: Spotted sea trout, redfish, king mackerel, snapper

species.name: King mackerel

species.name: Vermilion + red snapper

species.name: Cobia

patterns.title: Cobia migration sight-fishing
patterns.technique: Big bucktail jig 3-4 oz chartreuse; live pinfish
patterns.where: Inside the 4-7 ft sand shoreline; tower-boats run the beach scanning

species.name: Tarpon

patterns.details: Standard MI shallow-warmwater bass tactic. Frog at first light + last light; jig + worm midday under cover.
